DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF RAY ROSS'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY VERIFICATIONS AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION IS DENIED AS UNTIMELY PURSUANT TO CCP 2031.310(C). DEFECTS IN THE VERIFICATION ARE NOT TANTAMOUNT TO FAILING TO SERVE A VERIFICATION. COUNSEL FOR DEFENSE TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING WITH ADDITIONAL LANGUAGE STATING THE NON-APPEARANCE OF PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 DIRECTLY TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT JSKRMPOTIC@GMAIL.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEPHANIE KRMPOTIC, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Off Calendar Oct-19-2021 Off Calendar

Amended MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-23-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF RAY ROSS' Amended MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT

Plaintiff's amended motion for leave to file first amended complaint is granted on the condition that Plaintiff obtain a continuance of the trial date that would afford defendant sufficient time to file a motion for summary adjudication of the claim for punitive damages. The court believes that the stated condition is appropriate to avoid prejudice to defendant in light of the approaching November 15, 2021 trial date, particularly in light of plaintiff's lack of diligence in conducting discovery and delay in seeking leave to amend since discovering the alleged factual basis for the claim in April 2021. The Court declines to address defendant's argument that the amendment would be futile because the proposed amendment fails to allege a valid claim for punitive damages. (See California Casualty Gen. Ins. v. Superior Court (1985) 173 Cal.App.3d 274, 281 ["the preferable practice would be to permit the amendment and allow the parties to test its legal sufficiency by demurrer, motion for judgment on the pleadings or other appropriate proceedings."].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)

Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Discovery Verifications And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-29-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Discovery Verifications And Request For Monetary Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted. Plaintiff's motion to compel discovery verifications and request for monetary sanctions is denied. Plaintiff complains that Defendant Walgreen Co.'s written responses to plaintiff's form interrogatories and requests for production of documents were not signed under oath by the party to whom those discovery responses were directed, as required by the Code. (See Code Civ. Proc. secs.250(a), 2031.250(a).) When the party is a corporation, one of the party's officers or agents must sign the response under oath on that party's behalf. (Code Civ. Proc. secs. 2030.250(b), 2031.250(b).) Walgreen attached verifications from a person who, "being first duly sworn," attested that she was an employee authorized to make the verification on its behalf. The verification stated, "The information set forth in the foregoing [responses] was assembled by employees of Walgreen Co. from such corporation's records and files and from personnel in the appropriate offices and departments of such corporation, and the matters stated therein are true and correct to the best of my knowledge and belief. I have no personal knowledge of the facts contained in these responses, but am merely the representative of Walgreen Co. assigned responsibility for verifying these responses." (Ladva Decl., Ex. C.) Such a verification, when made on behalf of a corporate entity, substantially complies with the requirements of California law. (See California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (The Rutter Guide 2020) par. 8:1106 ["The person designated to verify the corporation's responses may not have personal knowledge of all facts stated therein. (The entity is obliged to provide facts known to any agent or employee, but the particular officer verifying may not have personal thereof.) Under such circumstances, 'information and belief' is the only realistic way to verify answers on behalf of the entity."].) As such, plaintiff's motion lacks merit, and is also untimely. (Code Civ. Proc. secs. 2030.300(c), 2031.310(c).)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/EPS)

Plaintiff'S Motion For Reconsideration [Moving Papers To Be Filed]
Off Calendar Jul-13-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. GURMAN S. BAL, Esq. of Ladva Law Firm (Tel: 415-296-8844) for plaintiff;
Atty. EMILY G. GENGE, Esq. of Burnham Brown (Tel: 510-444-6800) for defendants Walgreen Co. and Lawrence Wong.

Hearing Re: Plaintiff's Motion For Reconsideration.

Both sides stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem deciding plaintiff's motion for reconsideration as to the reconsideration only. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on this.

The issue of reconsideration is not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion for reconsideration is granted.

Court to sign formal order as to the reconsideration.

Plaintiff does not stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem deciding the underlying motion by plaintiff to compel verifications and request for monetary sanctions.

Matter discussed.

Matter continued to July 29, 2021 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, Law & Motion.

Judge Pro Tem to prepare and submit a report and recommendation.

Judge Pro Tem: Stephanie Krmpotic; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Verifications And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-14-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Emily Genge, Esq. 510-444-6800 of Burnham Brown for Defendant Walgreens.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested/Discussed. The Court indicates Plaintiff's Counsel contested the tentative ruling but did not appear for today's hearing. The Court having reviewed the pleadings rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF RAY ROSS's Motion To Compel Discovery Verifications And Request For Monetary Sanctions.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Plaintiff's motion is denied as untimely pursuant to CCP 2031.310(c). Defects in the verification are not tantamount to failing to serve a verification.

Counsel for Defense to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling with additional language stating the non-appearance of Plaintiff's Counsel in compliance with CRC 3.1312 directly to the Judge Pro Tem at jskrmpotic@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Stephanie Krmpotic, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
